hypothesis: If transfer learning from large chemical databases (ChEMBL and ZINC-15) plus fine-tuning on 103 Covalent Triazine Framework (CTF) structures, followed by reinforcement learning with expert-defined rewards for flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, and pattern matching, is effective, then the generated CTF candidates will exhibit higher validity, uniqueness, internal diversity, novelty, and expert-filter pass rates and lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance than models without such transfer learning and RL optimization.
